div.head: J. H. Vries
div.p: Most Illustrious Excellency: I have the honor of sending you a copy of my letter to Mrs. Lincoln and the illustrious nation whose destinies are intrusted to your hands. You will see in it the faithful expression of my sentiments for the great statesman Abraham Lincoln. You will find, at the same time, an antidote to the plague which threatens America by the crime of Dr. Blackburn.
I do not doubt but the papers will make known this remedy by publication, and thus snatch from certain death thousands of victims, if the dreadful plague should break out.
May God give you the light to lead the great nation of the United States to its highest destiny, is my most cordial wish for you, whom Providence has called to the eminent post of President.
Deign to accept the humble homage of the distinguished sentiments with which I have the honor to be your excellency’s very humble and most obedient servant,
